name: modal
description: A centred modal dialog (shadcn Dialog), STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. Built on the native HTML <dialog> element: the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (declarative open, no script, like popovertarget) and every close path (header x, backdrop, Escape) is a native <form method="dialog"> submit / the browser's modal default. The browser renders it in the top layer, traps focus, paints the ::backdrop, and exposes it as a modal dialog to AT, so the partial is CSP-clean with no enhancer. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby). Body + footer are OWNED Content slots, never <slot>. The presentation shell the kit's modal stack renders through: it carries the io.lievit.kit.component.ModalView affordances (closeButton, closeByClickingAway, closeByEscaping, width) and io.lievit.kit.ModalConfig chrome (heading/description, width xs..7xl/full/screen mirroring the kit Width/ModalWidth tokens, start/center alignment, submit/cancel footer slot). The trigger-driven static counterpart of the server-state-held wire dialog (registry/wire/dialog/). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens.
docs: Copies the modal partial to your JTE root at lievit/modal.jte, reached as @template.lievit.modal(...). Pass a unique `id` (the commandfor target), the `trigger` content (label/icon) and the `content` modal body (server-rendered, never a <slot>); add an optional `heading` + `description` (a labelled dialog: role=dialog + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby) and a `footer` slot for Submit/Cancel actions. Tune `width` (xs|sm|md|lg|xl|2xl..7xl|full|screen, the kit Width/ModalWidth tokens), `alignment` ("start" | "center"), `closeButton`, `closeByClickingAway`. The browser owns open/close (native <dialog> showModal + Escape + focus-trap + return-focus) so there is no JS and no enhancer to wire. The source is yours: edit freely. For a modal whose open-state lives server-side (the server decides open/closed and re-renders), use the wire dialog component instead.
